So, first is a split test, and this is what most people do, so a split test is very simple, page A versus page B, or you can actually do page A versus page B versus page C, in our case, with our particular system, depends on the system, up to 10 pages, but that's a split test, where you have a complete page, usually versus another complete page. A single variable test will be where you take one element on the page, a variable being the headline, a variable being the call to action, or the hero image, or whatever it is on the page that you see, and you just take it, so headline one versus headline two versus headline three, that's referred to as a single variable test. A multi-variate is when you start getting a little more sophisticated, instead of testing one element versus one element, you can now test multiple elements on the same page. So you can test at the same time, headline one versus headline two versus headline three, at the same time you're testing call to action one versus call to action two versus call to action three, and image one versus image two versus image three.
